definition: Supply-driven term premium increases are qualitatively worse for both credit and equity markets than expectations-driven rate increases. Supply-driven rises raise discount rates without reflecting improved growth prospects, producing pure valuation compression. Confirmed across 2013 Taper Tantrum, 2018 Q4, and 2023 Q3 episodes.
evidence: Three confirming episodes (2013, 2018 Q4, 2023 Q3) showed S&P declines with minimal earnings deterioration
Credit-specific: supply-driven term premium raises CLO AAA yields 1:1 without improving borrower cash flows
pair_1 debate confirmed as 'one of the highest-confidence shared findings'
open_questions: Can supply-driven episodes be identified in real-time with sufficient lead time to be actionable?
How does the 30-50bp decomposition uncertainty affect identification of supply-driven moves?
Is the small sample of clean supply-driven episodes sufficient for robust inference?
